hypothesis: Applying combined representation-, fragment-, and string-based diversity constraints (10% similarity, 5% fragment, 3% 10‑gram) together with adaptive penalization during REINVENT4 reinforcement learning for singlet fission optimization will produce generated molecule sets with higher chemical diversity (more unique fragments, more clusters, lower mean pairwise similarity, and fewer overrepresented patterns) over 1000 iterations while maintaining comparable singlet fission ΔE_SF scores compared to unconstrained training.
